feedback.title: Feedback!

donation.title: Keep Chatvote alive with your donation!
donation.description: We are currently funding Chatvote entirely out of our own pocket. Your donation helps us maintain this project and cover server and AI costs.

guide.title: What can I do with chatvote?

intro.description: chatvote is an interactive AI tool that helps you learn about the positions and projects of parties. You can ask the AI assistant questions about various political topics, and it will provide neutral answers based on the programs and other publications of the parties.
intro.step2: chatvote searches relevant documents like programs to find the appropriate information.
intro.step3: The relevant information is then used to generate an understandable and neutral answer.
intro.step4: You can then have the party's position evaluated by clicking the button below the answer. Or have the answer analyzed based on the party's voting behavior.

questions.title: Questions
questions.description: In principle, you can ask all the questions you have about party programs or their positions. Additionally, you can also ask questions about general topics like the course of an election.
partyExamples: What is LFI's position on climate?
How does RN position itself on public debt?
How does Renaissance want to reduce bureaucracy?
How do the Ecologists want to advance the digital transition?
How do PS and PCF want to implement working time reform?
How do EELV and Renaissance want to improve European cooperation?
compareExamples: How do parties differ in the fight against climate change?
What are the position differences between Renaissance and RN on debt?
Compare the positions of LFI and RN on migration.
generalExamples: How can I vote by proxy?

numberOfParties.title: Number of parties
numberOfParties.question: How many parties can I chat with?
numberOfParties.description: You can start the chat with up to {max} parties simultaneously, but you have the option to add more parties during the conversation.
numberOfParties.addMore: Additionally, you can easily add or remove parties from the chat via the button above the text field.

evaluatePosition.title: Evaluate position
evaluatePosition.description: When you click this button below one of the messages, the position of the message is evaluated. The following criteria are considered: Feasibility, Short-term and long-term effects.
evaluatePosition.perplexityInfo: We use current information and sources from the Internet provided by Perplexity.ai.

votingBehavior.title: Analyze voting behavior
votingBehavior.description: When you click this button below one of the messages, the party's past voting behavior is analyzed. Additionally, by clicking "Show votes" you can see detailed information about the party's votes.

data.title: Data sources
data.question: What data is used?
data.description: To provide well-founded and neutral answers, chatvote uses a variety of data sources:
data.programsDescription: In addition to election programs, basic programs and other documents from parties are used to get a complete picture of party positions.
data.positionPapersDescription: In addition to basic programs, election programs, position papers and other party documents are used to get a complete picture of positions.
data.internetSourcesDescription: For differentiated position evaluation, chatvote uses the Perplexity.ai service, which relies on quality Internet sources like news sites.
data.sourcesLink: We have listed all the sources that chatvote uses here.

guidelines.title: Guidelines
guidelines.question: What guidelines does chatvote follow in its answers?
guidelines.description: The following guidelines apply to answers in chats:
guidelines.sourceBasedDescription: Answers must be based on relevant statements from the provided program excerpts.
guidelines.neutralityDescription: Party positions must be presented neutrally and without judgment.
guidelines.transparencyDescription: For each statement, relevant sources must be directly linked to allow detailed examination and content verification.

partySelection.title: Party selection
partySelection.question: What criteria are used to select parties?
partySelection.description: The initial selection of parties was made based on the publication of their election programs. We now want to gradually add missing parties and are very happy for your help.
partySelection.missingParty: If you are missing a party, feel free to send us an email with their program in PDF attached to info@chatvote.org, and we will add it as soon as possible.

electionFlow.dialogueWith: I engage with candidates and parties
electionFlow.askQuestions: Ask your questions about candidates and parties at the national level.
electionFlow.noCandidates: No candidates registered for this municipality.
electionFlow.noCandidatesYet: There are no candidates registered yet for {municipality}.
electionFlow.selectCandidatesDescription: Select one or more candidates to discuss their program with the AI.
electionFlow.municipalityPrompt: Before asking your question, enter your municipality or postal code
electionFlow.searchHint: Search for your municipality by name or postal code.

aiDisclaimer.title: AI Notice
aiDisclaimer.intro: The answers on chatvote are generated by artificial intelligence. They are based on information extracted from publicly accessible party programs and election programs. While chatvote strives to provide accurate information about party positions and values, it should be noted:
aiDisclaimer.automated: The processing and generation of all content is automated.
aiDisclaimer.notOfficial: The answers should not be considered as official party statements.
aiDisclaimer.complexPositions: Complex political positions may not be captured in all their nuances.
aiDisclaimer.inaccuracies: Inaccuracies or misinterpretations may sometimes occur.
aiDisclaimer.educationalTool: This AI chat serves as an educational tool for discovering different political positions. For binding information, please use official party sources.

donateResult.successDescription: Thank you very much for your support! We will use your donation to enable neutral and independent information.
donateResult.failed: Unfortunately, your donation was not successful.
